Notice Of Continued Motion To Consolidate For Trial The Cases Against Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. And Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc. Formerly Known As Kaiser Cement Corporation
Off Calendar Feb-19-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Andrew Chew of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs.
Andrew Sharp of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendants Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc.
CJ Manoli of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendants Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc.
Laura Przetak of SPANOS | PRZETAK (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel.

RULING: PLAINTIFFS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE FOR TRIAL THE CASES AGAINST KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC. AND HANSON PERMANENTE CEMENT, INC. FORMERLY KNOWN AS KAISER CEMENT CORPORATION
Parties stipulated to have all matters heard simultaneously. Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion to consolidate for trial the cases against Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc. formerly known as Kaiser Cement corporation, into the following subgroups: 1) wrongful death/mesothelioma; 2) living/lung cancer: 3) wrongful death/lung cancer; 4) living/other cancer; 5) wrongful death/other cancer; 6) living/asbestosis; 7) wrongful death/asbestosis; 8) living/pleural disease, is denied. The actions do not involve sufficient common questions of law or fact, economy and convenience would not be served by a joint trial, and consolidation may prejudice one or both of the defendants. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed order in compliance with C.R.C. 3.1312. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR #13293 spham28@yahoo.com (Tel: 415-517-5439) (503/CML)

Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendants Aladdin Heating Corporation, And Marine Engineering And Supply Company
Off Calendar Sep-11-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendants Aladdin Heating Corporation and Marine Engineering and Supply Company.
Dept. 514. Judge: Eric R. Fleming. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703.

James H. Ghilotti, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs William Bolls and Nancy Bolls, who were not present at this morning's hearing.
No appearance by defaulted defendants Aladdin Heating Corporation or Marine Engineering and Supply Company.
Plaintiffs' complaint was filed on November 23, 2015 and served on defendant Aladdin Heating Corporation on March 8, 2016.
Statement of Damages was served on defendant Aladdin Heating Corporation on March 8, 2016 and filed on May 5, 2016.
Default was entered against defendant Aladdin Heating Corporation on May 5, 2016.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was filed on August 9, 2018.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiffs William Bolls and Nancy Bolls, and against the Defaulted Defendant Aladdin Heating Corporation, as follows:

Economic Damages for plaintiff William Bolls - $1,078,700.79.
Non-Economic Damages for plaintiff William Bolls - $10,000,000.00.
Loss of Consortium as to plaintiff Nancy Bolls - $10,000,000.00.
Total Economic & Non-Economic Damages as to defendant Aladdin Heating Corporation - $21,078,700.79.

Plaintiffs' complaint was filed on November 23, 2015 and served on defendant Marine Engineering and Supply Company on April 13, 2016.
Statement of Damages was served on defendant Marine Engineering and Supply Company on April 13, 2016 and filed on May 10, 2016.
Default was entered against defendant Marine Engineering and Supply Company on May 10, 2016.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was filed on August 9, 2018.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiffs William Bolls and Nancy Bolls, and against the Defaulted Defendant Marine Engineering and Supply Company, as follows:

Economic Damages for plaintiff William Bolls - $1,078,700.79.
Non-Economic Damages for plaintiff Williams Bolls - $10,000,000.00.
Loss of Consortium as to plaintiff Nancy Bolls - $10,000,000.00.
Total Economic & Non-Economic Damages as to defendant Marine Engineering and Supply Company - $21,078,700.79.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
